Fnrn Polrrrcnl PRACrrcEs ConaMrssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 r Sacramenro, CA g5gt4_232g
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
November 7,2008
Maryanne Vancio
Department of Parks and Recreation
County of San Diego
9150 Chesapeake Dr., Suite 200
San Diego, CA92123
Re
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-08-173
Dear Ms. Vancio:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Beciuse the question you pose
relates to past conduct and seeks general advice, we are issuing informal assistance as
allowed by Section 831 14 and Regulation 18329.2 Please note that our advice is based
solely on the provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the application, if
any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of interlit or
Government Code Section 1090.
QUESTTON
Would you have a conflict of interest in future decisions affecting clevelopment
applications regarding real property that is near property on which you rJside, deipite not
having any ownership interest in your residence?
' The Political Retirrm Act is contained in Government.Code Sections g1000 through 91014. All
statutory ref'erences are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulation-s of the Fair
Political Practices commission are contained in Sections I 8 I l0 rhrough I 8997 of Title 2 of the Califrrrnia
Code of Regulations" All regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2. Division 6 of the Califirrnia Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
r lnf<lrmal assistance does not provide the requeskrr with the imnrunity prorided by an opinion or
tbrmal written advice. (Section 8311,1; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


Our File No. I-08-173
Page 2
CONCLUSION
Generally "no." If you do not have an "interest in real property" as defined in the
Act and do not have any other economic interest that will be impattedby a decision, you
will not have a conflict of interest under the Act.
FACTS
You are an employee of the county of San Diego at the Department of parks and
Recreation. As County Trails Program Coordinator, you review ail discretionary
development applications to determine if the project applicant should be required to
dedicate an easement for a trail on the property. You ieview the location of the property
relative to proposed trail alignments shown on adopted community and/or regional trail
maps. The applicant may be required to dedicate a trail easement if the landior which
the approval is sought includes some or the entire trail corridor.
You stated that you reside in the.o,rniy. You do not own a residence, but reside
with your "significant other." You are not registered domestic partners and you do not
pay rent. You do not receive income from your significant other's business.
Section 87r.09 prohibitsa,, *il::r:":j;m making, participaring in making,
or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental deiision in
which the official has a financial interest.
The Commission has adopted an eighrstep standard analysis for deciding whether
an official has a disqualifying conflict of interest. (Regulation f SZOO(U).) The general
rule, however, is that a conflict of interest exists whenever a public official makes a
governmental decision, which has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on
one or more of his or her financial interests.
Step 1. Are you a "public official,,?
Section 82048 defines a public official as "every member, officer, employee or
consultant of a state or local government agency." As an employee of the County of San
Diego, you are a public official.
Step 2. Will you be making, participating in making or influencing a governmental
decision?
A public official "makes a govemmental decision" when the official, acting
within the authority of his or her office or position, votes on a matter, obligates or
commits his or her agency to any corrse of action, or enters into any contrictual


Our File No. I-08-173
Page 3
agreement on behalf of his or her agency. (Regulation 18702.1.) $ public official
"participates in a governmental decision" when, acting within the auihority of his or her
position and without significant substantive or intervening review, the official negotiates,
advises, or makes recommendations to the decisionmakeiregarding the governmental
decision. (Regulation 15702.2.) A public official is attempting to -r.. hil or her official
position to influence a governmental decision if, for the purpose of influencing the
decision, the official contacts or appears before or otherwise attempts to influence, any
member, officer, employee, or consultant of his or her agency. (Regulation tg7OZ.3.)
You stated that in your position as County Trails Program Coordinator you
recommend whether a project applicant should dedicate a trail easement as a condition of
approval for a discretionary development project. This would be considereJ participating
in a governmental decision.
step 3. Do You Have a potentiarty Disqualifying Economic Interest?
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to conflicts of interest arising
from certain enumerated economic interests. Theii economic interests are described in
Section 87103 and Regulations 18703-1g703.5, inclusive:
o I public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or she
has a direct or indirect investment of $2,000 or more. (Section g7103(a);
Regulation I 8703. l(a).)
o { public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or she
is a director, officer, partner, trustee, emproyee, or holds any position of
management. (Section 97103(d); Regulation 1g703.1(b).)
o A public official has an economic interest in real property in which he or she has a
direct or indirecr inrerest of $2,000 or more. lSeciion gZiO:(U); Regulation
18703.2.) Section 82033 provides that an "interest in real property';includes any
leasehold, beneficial, or ownership interest in real propertt tocatea in the
jurisdiction owned directly, indirectly, or beneficiuity Uy t-he public official if the
fair market value of the interest is $2,000 or more. Iio*"u"r, the terms .,interest
in real property" and "leasehold interest" do not include the interest of a tenant in
a periodic tenancy of one month or less. (Regulation rgz33.)
o An official has an economic interest in any source of income, including promised
income, totaling $500 or more within l2 months prior to the decision. "(Section
87 103(c); Regulation 1 8703.3.)
o d public official has an economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if
the gifts total $390 or more within l2 months piio. to the dJcision. (Section
87 I 03(e); Regulation t8703.4.)


Our File No. I-08-173
Page 4
A public official has an economic interest in his or her personal expenses, income,
assets, or liabilities, as well as those of his or her immediate family. This is
commonly referred to as the "personal financial effects" rule. (Section 87103;
Regulation 18703.5.)
As you describe the facts, you do not have any of the specified economic
interests. You do not own or lease real property, you do not have a source of income
(other than the county), nor do you have any sources of gifts3 at issue in connection with
the decision. You have not described any business positions or investments. Moreover,
you have not identified any effect on your personal finances.
Absent an economic interest in the decision as defined in the Act, a conflict of
interest will not exist. Of course, this analysis may change depending on the facts and
you should contact us if you need assistance regarding a specific prospective decision.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By:
W. Wallace
General Counsel
Division
Jwwjgl
r The Commission has advised that personal gifts received within an established bona fide dating
relationshiparenotgiftsundertheAct. Thistypeofrelationshipissimilartoafamilial 
orspousal
relationship. in whish people frequently exchange personal gifis and disclosure or disqualific'ation would
not further the purposes of the Act. (Albuquerque Advice Letter, No. A-00-l 20: DeRisa Advice Letter.
No. I-98-284; sftea Advice Lemer. No. A-84-085: also see Secrion 9202g(bx3).)
